sections.instruction: Complete the form below. Write ONE WORD OR A NUMBER.

Which TWO things does Alice say about the Dolphin Conservation Trust?
options: A Children make up most of the membership
B It’s the country’s largest conservation organisation
C It helps finance campaigns for changes in fishing practices
D It employs several dolphin experts full-time
E Volunteers help in various ways

questions.content: Why is Alice so pleased the Trust has won the Charity Commission award?
options: A It has brought in extra money
B It made the work of the trust better known
C It has attracted more members.

questions.content: Alice says oil exploration causes problems to dolphins because of
options: A noise
B oil leaks
C movement of ships

questions.content: Alice became interested in dolphins when
options: A she saw one swimming near her home
B she heard a speaker at her school
C she read a book about them
A Moondancer
B Echo
C Kiwi
D Samson

questions.content: What helped Rob to prepare to play the character of a doctor?
options: A the stories his grandfather told him
B the times when he watched his grandfather working
C the way he imagined his grandfather at work

questions.content: In the play’s first scene, the boredom of village life was suggested by
options: A repetition of words and phrases
B scenery painted in dull colours
C long pauses within conversations

questions.content: What has Rob learned about himself through working in a group?
options: A He likes to have clear guidelines
B He copes well with stress
C He thinks he is a good leader

questions.content: To support the production, research material was used which described
options: A political developments
B changing social attitudes
C economic transformations

questions.content: What problem did the students overcome in the final rehearsal?
options: A one person forgetting their words
B an equipment failure
C the injury of one character
A be on time
B get a letter of recommendation
C plan for the final year
D make sure the institution’s focus is relevant
E show ability in Theatre Studies
F make travel arrangements and bookings
G ask for help

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY.
sections.passage_or_context:
‘Self-regulatory focus theory’ and leadership
Self-regulatory focus theory
People’s focus is to approach pleasure or avoid pain
Promotion goals focus on {{BLANK}}
Prevention goals emphasise avoiding punishment
Factors that affect people’s focus
The Chronic Factor
• comes from one’s {{BLANK}}
The {{BLANK}} Factor
• we are more likely to focus on promotion goals when with a {{BLANK}}
• we are more likely to focus on prevention goals with our boss
How people’s focus affects them
Promotion Focus: People think about an ideal version of themselves, their {{BLANK}} and their gains.
Prevention Focus: People think about their ‘ought’ self and their obligations
Leaders
Leadership behaviour and {{BLANK}} affects people’s focus
Transformational Leaders:
• pay special attention to the {{BLANK}} of their followers
• passionately communicate a clear {{BLANK}}
• inspire promotion focus in followers
Transactional Leaders:
• create {{BLANK}} to make expectations clear
• emphasise the results of a mistake
• inspire prevention focus in followers
Conclusion
Promotion Focus is good for jobs requiring {{BLANK}}
Prevention Focus is good for work such as a surgeon Leaders’ actions affect which focus people use.
